description:
Your current working directory is /home/user.
    
    Background  
    -----------
    You are developing a tiny helper script that needs to keep track of how much space its own workspace occupies.  
    A folder named /home/user/utils_workspace already exists and contains a few very small text files.  
    Right now the only thing missing is a one–line **disk-usage report** that the rest of the tool-chain can read.

    What you must do  
    ----------------
    1. Calculate the **total apparent size in bytes** of every file that is located **directly inside**  
       /home/user/utils_workspace (there are no sub-directories, so a simple directory-wide total is enough).  
       • “Apparent size” means byte-for-byte size of file contents (as reported by  
         `du --apparent-size -b` or a similar method), not the on-disk block usage.  

    2. Create a new text file named  
       /home/user/utils_workspace/disk_usage_report.txt  

    3. Write **exactly one line** to that file, using this template (no extra spaces or blank lines):  
       Total apparent size of utils_workspace: <NUMBER> bytes  

       Replace <NUMBER> with the byte total you determined in step 1.  
       • The line must end with a single Unix newline (LF).  
       • Nothing else should be written to the file.

    4. Do **not** move, delete or modify any other file inside /home/user/utils_workspace.

    Verification notes  
    ------------------
    Automated tests will:
    • Read /home/user/utils_workspace/disk_usage_report.txt  
    • Expect to find exactly the template line shown above with a **single integer** in place of <NUMBER>.  
      No thousands separators, no decimal points, no surrounding quotes.  
    • Confirm that the file contains exactly one line and terminates with a newline character.

    Hints (not mandatory)  
    ----------------------
    • Either a single `du` invocation or a pipeline ending in a redirection is enough to complete the task.  
    • Remember to use byte units, not the default block-sized output.  
    • Check your result with `cat` before finishing.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing environment
        ------------------------
        Directory /home/user/utils_workspace/ already contains three files
        that the agent must NOT alter:

        1. /home/user/utils_workspace/script1.sh
           Contents (36 bytes):
             #!/bin/bash\n
             echo "Utility script 1"\n

        2. /home/user/utils_workspace/script2.sh
           Contents (36 bytes):
             #!/bin/bash\n
             echo "Utility script 2"\n

        3. /home/user/utils_workspace/README.md
           Contents (22 bytes):
             # Utilities Workspace\n

        Verified apparent sizes:
            script1.sh  ─ 36
            script2.sh  ─ 36
            README.md   ─ 22
            -------------------
            TOTAL       ─ 94  (bytes)

        Expected artefact
        -----------------
        After successful completion, the agent must have created:

        /home/user/utils_workspace/disk_usage_report.txt
            Exact contents (including trailing newline):
            ---------------------------------------------
            Total apparent size of utils_workspace: 94 bytes\n
            ---------------------------------------------

        The automated checker will read that file and compare the
        entire byte sequence to the line above.
    -->
